title: Lastrea grisea
taxonomy: Lastrea grisea is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is native to the Himalayas, specifically found in the northern regions of India, Nepal, and Bhutan. The classification of Lastrea grisea places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Athyriales. Within the family Athyriaceae, Lastrea grisea is further grouped into the subfamily Athyrioideae and tribe Athyrieae. The classification of Lastrea grisea continues to be refined as new research and data become available.

intro: Lastrea grisea, also known as the grey-leaved lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage.
